observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management explore alternative water sources in order to resume the operation of the fish hatchery project and support the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources’ eel industry development project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer conducted site validation and assessed that the water source of the fish hatchery has been eroded. Moreover, the concrete showed cracks and water outflow because of the soil movement during the onslaught of typhoon Rosita. The assessment also includes that a feasibility study be conducted if the restoration of the project is still viable considering the cost-benefit analysis. The report was submitted to the Mayor’s Office for appropriate action. This was further endorsed by the Mayor’s Office to the concerned offices to assess if the fish hatchery project is still viable.
observations.reason: No feasibility study yet was prepared by the Management to determine if the restoration of the fish hatchery project is still feasible.

observations.recommendation:
8. We have recommended and Management agreed to appropriate  an  amount
        to cover the increase  in  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and  Assistant
        Municipal   Treasurer’s   fidelity    bond    to    include    their
        accountabilities as disbursing  officers  and  that  of  accountable
        forms, and pay its corresponding bond premium in accordance with the
        Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019;  to  ensure  the
        recovery of loss in cases of defalcations, shortages and  unrelieved
        losses.

7. We have recommended and Management agreed to  strictly  comply  with
        the imposition of warranty security for the purchases  of  goods  to
        insure  that  manufacturing  defects  shall  be  corrected  by   the
        suppliers within the period provided in GPPB Resolution No.  30-2017
        dated July 31, 2017.

9. We have recommended and the Bids  and  Awards  Committee  agreed  to
        provide clear and specific information in the bidding  documents  as
        basis of the prospective bidders in preparing their bids pursuant to
        the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184
        and  6th  Edition  of  the  Philippine  Bidding  Documents  for  the
        Procurement of Goods, to  avoid  confusion  which  would  result  in
        failure of bidding.

3. We have recommended and Management agreed to deduct  the  amount  of
        ₱41,126.75, from the payment of the project  to  the  contractor  as
        liquidated damages pursuant to PRDP Module  1:  Guidelines  for  the
        Procurement of Infrastructure Subprojects upon receipt of  the  fund
        from the DA – PRDP.

7. We have recommended and Management agreed to require the  contractor
        to cause the extension of the validity of the  performance  security
        to cover  the  defects  liability  period,  one  year  from  project
        completion to final acceptance, pursuant to the pertinent provisions
        of PRDP Module I: Guidelines for the Procurement  of  Infrastructure
        Subprojects.

We have recommended and Management agreed to create an Inventory Committee that will conduct the inventory taking annually for all the property owned/controlled by the Municipality; and to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property and Equipment in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006, and furnish a copy to the COA Auditor.
observations.managementAction: E.O. No. 08, s. 2021 was issued by the Municipal Mayor institutionalizing the Inventory Committee of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to strictly monitor the completeness of information in the signboards, to encourage public participation in the monitoring of implementation of the various projects of the Municipality, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office strictly monitored the completeness of information in the signboards

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, ensure that the Annual Procurement Plan and Supplemental Annual Procurement Plan contain the complete information provided in Section 7.3.4 of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 and shall be in accordance with the format prescribed by the GPPB.
observations.managementAction: The APP for CY 2022 was prepared in accordance with the format prescribed by GPPB

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to submit the lacking documents and ensure that the required documents for procurements through negotiated procurement (emergency cases) are complete before payments are made in accordance with the provisions of the GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, to avoid disallowance; and to ensure that the Omnibus Sworn Statement is in accordance with the latest format prescribed in the same Circular.
observations.managementAction: The lacking documents were submitted to the COA Office, and the Omnibus Sworn Statements attached in the procurements in CY 2021 were in accordance with the latest format issued by the GPPB.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to provide an amount of ₱500,000.00 to be deposited in the LGU Equity account in accordance with Section 5.03 of the Implementation Management Agreement to ensure sufficiency of the fund for the implementation of the Mountain Province Heirloom Rice Consolidation, Processing and Marketing Enterprise’s civil work; and to prevent suspension of subsequent releases of the grant by the Department of Agriculture and non-implementation of the project.
observations.managementAction: The amount of ₱500,000.00 was transferred to PRDP LGU Equity account under JEV No. 300-2111-004 in November 2021

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to revert the accumulated engineering overhead of the General Fund proper and Trust Fund totalling ₱223,530.30 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund for appropriation to other priority programs, projects and activities, and the 20% Development Fund amounting to ₱249,157.00 for other priority development projects.
observations.managementAction: JEV Nos. 100-2103-268 and 100-2103-264 were drawn to revert the amounts in March 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw an entry to adjust the erroneous recording of the transactions and to recognize the corresponding depreciations of the equipment

We have recommended further and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the transactions on their proper accounts for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
JEV Nos. 100-2102-165 to 168 were drawn to adjust the erroneous recording of the equipment in March 2021.

Pieces of equipment purchased in calendar year 2021 were recorded in their proper PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, prepare the 1) Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-kind Donations; 2)Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; 3)Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods; and 4)Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated PPE; and furnish the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team within ten  working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: The Report on the Receipt and Utilization/Issuance of Donated PPE and In-kind donations was prepared and copy furnished COA. No cash donation was received by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly monitor the implementation of the project to ensure that this is implemented within the construction schedule to avoid delays in its completion.
observations.managementAction: The project was fully implemented in September 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and management agreed to:

a.	except for the Notices of Disallowance under appeal, notify in writing, all persons identified to be liable for audit disallowances and request the settlement of their obligations; and

b.	enforce the withholding of salaries or other compensation due to persons liable in accordance with COA Orders of Execution issued.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were sent to persons liable and some are already paying their disallowances. A total of ₱7,785.40 was refunded by the persons liable in calendar year 2021
observations.reason: Most of the persons liable are out from gov’t services and some are deceased.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management cause the transfer of the parcel of land purchased in May 2017 to minimize risks of unlawful occupants and third party claims.
observations.managementAction: An amount was already budgeted in CY 2022 for the payment of estate tax. Moreover, the Management is coordinating with the owner’s children for the submission of the requirements such as TIN. Based on MOA dated November 21, 2016, expenses necessary and incidental for the transfer of the property will be shouldered by the LGU.
observations.reason: The processing of the transfer of the parcel of land is ongoing

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management henceforth, require the BAC Secretariat to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within fourteen calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: Procurement Monitoring Report for 1st Semester of calendar year 2021 was submitted to GPPB on August 4, 2021 duly acknowledged by the GPPB-TSO.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management instruct the implementing offices to fast track project implementation and henceforth, monitor and evaluate the implementation of the Programs, Projects and Activities.
observations.managementAction: All Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) funded under the 20% DF and 5% MDRRMF in CY 2017 were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management complete the required membership composition of its MSWMB to include the required sectoral representative from NGOs whose principal purpose is to promote recycling and the protection of air and water quality and to also conduct a survey on these individuals/organizations who undertaker cycling, manufacturing and packaging activities to assist them in formally registering their businesses so that they can be duly represented in the MSWMB.
observations.managementAction: E.O. No. 06, s. 2021 was issued by the Municipal Mayor reorganizing the MSWMB to include CSO representatives and their duties and responsibilities

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management provide a garage for its motor vehicles to safeguard the heavy equipment and motor vehicles with a book value of ₱7,548,481.00 pursuant to Section 361.e of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The papers for the transfer of the lot, to be utilized as motorpool of the Municipality, through deed of donation from a private individual is submitted at the Registry of Deeds. For the building, this will be transferred by the DPWH to the Municipality after its completion
observations.reason: The transfer of the lot to the Municipality is on-going

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management resolve the site problem and utilize the funds pursuant to the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the donor agency.
observations.managementAction:
The request for the amendment of project title, from “Construction of One-unit Comfort Room w/ Water Supply and One-unit View Deck” to “One-unit Pathway and Maintenance going to the Summit of Mount Amuyao”, was approved by the GMA Network, Inc. through Secretary’s Certificate signed by Roberto Rafael V. Lucila, Corporate Secretary.

The implementation of the project is already ongoing.
